title: PPA Secrets Manifest
description: Declares the secret names and metadata for a single bot principal. Values are never stored here — only key names and scopes. This file is encrypted as secrets-manifest.enc (JWE RFC 7516) before being committed to a bot repo.

bot_id.description: The canonical bot identifier in kebab-case (e.g. 'legal-bot'). Maps to the BOT_NAME segment of canonical key names after normalization to UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

version.description: Semantic version of this manifest. Increment when secrets are added, removed, or scope changes.

secrets.description: Ordered list of secret declarations. Required secrets must resolve at startup; optional secrets are omitted from the bundle if unavailable.

blocked.description: Canonical key names (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE) this bot is constitutionally forbidden from requesting. Enforced by SecretManifestResolver before any vault call. Cannot be overridden by delegation.

app_name.description: The application or service that owns this credential (e.g. 'westlaw', 'anthropic'). Used to construct the canonical key name: {BOT_NAME}__{APP_NAME}__{KEY_NAME}.

key.description: The key name in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. Combined with bot_id and app_name to form the canonical name: LEGAL_BOT__WESTLAW__WESTLAW_API_KEY.

required.description: When true, a missing or unresolvable secret must fail the bot's startup. When false, the secret is omitted from the bundle if unavailable.

scopes.description: Fine-grained capability labels this secret unlocks. Used for audit logging and delegation scope enforcement.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this key unlocks and why this bot needs it. Not used at runtime; informational only.
examples: LexisNexis case law search API for US federal courts

rotation_days.description: Expected rotation interval in days. Informational — does not enforce rotation; use vault policies for enforcement.
